title: Show
description: A persistent content program that produces episodes over time. Declared in the publisher's adagents.json and referenced by products via shows selectors. Use distribution identifiers for cross-seller matching.

show_id.description: Publisher-assigned identifier for this show. Declared in the publisher's adagents.json shows array. Products reference shows via shows selectors with publisher_domain and show_ids. Use distribution identifiers for cross-seller matching across publishers.

genre.description: Genre tags. When genre_taxonomy is present, values are taxonomy IDs (e.g., IAB Content Taxonomy 3.0 codes). Otherwise free-form.

genre_taxonomy.description: Taxonomy system for genre values (e.g., 'iab_content_3.0'). When present, genre values should be valid taxonomy IDs. Recommended for machine-readable brand safety evaluation.

content_rating.description: Baseline content rating for the show. Individual episodes may override this.

cadence.description: How frequently the show releases new episodes

season.description: Current or most recent season identifier (e.g., '3', '2026', 'spring_2026'). A lightweight label — not a full season object.

production_quality.description: Production quality tier. Seller-declared. Maps to OpenRTB content.prodq (professional=1, prosumer=2, ugc=3).

talent.description: Hosts, recurring cast, creators associated with the show. Each talent entry may include a brand_url linking to their brand.json identity.

distribution.description: Where this show is distributed. Each entry maps the show to a publisher platform with platform-specific identifiers. Shows SHOULD include at least one platform-independent identifier (imdb_id, gracenote_id, eidr_id) when available.

related_shows.description: Relationships to other shows (spin-offs, companion shows, etc.). Each entry references another show by show_id within the same publisher's adagents.json.

show_id.description: The related show's show_id within this seller's response
